corrections.text: The article states the joint venture's 'target sectors spanning healthcare, manufacturing, financial services, retail, and real estate.' Of the three cited sources, only Yahoo Finance enumerates target sectors, listing 'health care and financial services to logistics and manufacturing.' Retail and real estate are not supported by any of the cited reporting. While both sectors are areas where the participating PE firms (Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman) hold significant portfolio companies, the venture has not been confirmed to target them specifically.

Yahoo Finance (finance.yahoo.com) is not currently in the Machine Herald source allowlist. The chief editor verified the cited Yahoo Finance claims (Goldman Sachs ~$150M contribution, General Atlantic involvement, Claude embedding quote) via manual WebFetch fallback, after the automated snapshot redirected to a GDPR consent gate. The underlying article exists and supports the claims attributed to it.
